summary.intent: Address two rounds of Codex automated review findings on PR #727 in fogodev/ars-ui, fixing bugs in Leptos/Dioxus Checkbox, HiddenInput, Label, and Root components, then get CI green and Codex approval.
summary.outcome: Both review-fix commits (f043fd68 and a second follow-up) were pushed, all 15 Codex threads were replied to and resolved, CI went fully green, and the second-pass commit was staged/committed with Codex co-author trailer.

repo: The repo uses a `@codex review` PR comment to retrigger Codex automated review after resolving threads.

Adapter-parity lint checks verify all implemented adapter components are reported; widget-demo lint checks must confirm the component name appears in category files (case-insensitive, snake/kebab/Pascal variants).
Core machine hook runs pending effects via `use_machine`, so adapters should wire callbacks into core `Props::on_checked_change` rather than manually duplicating click/change/reset callback calls.

Leptos/Dioxus callbacks are framework-local and not `Send + Sync`, so they cannot be wired directly into agnostic core props — a small `Api::default_checked()` getter is the correct bridge for adapter reset callbacks.
The `cargo xfmt`, `cargo xclippy`, and `cargo xtask lint adapter-parity` commands are the canonical local verification pipeline before committing.
Wasm compile checks use `cargo +nightly test --tests --target wasm32-unknown-unknown` with `--no-run`; Leptos uses `--features csr`, Dioxus uses `--features web`.
Styled visual color-delta assertions in the E2E widget smoke check should only be enforced for CSS/Tailwind examples, not plain unstyled widget examples.

code.finding: Core already has controlled-reset semantics including default_checked state; adapters were not using it, causing reset callbacks to silently do nothing in controlled mode.
Leptos Checkbox was not using prop:checked for reactive property binding and had duplicate manual callback invocations that should be delegated to core machine effects.
Dioxus Checkbox reset listener needed to call the adapter on_checked_change callback with Api::default_checked() value after dispatching Event::Reset.
Framework context helpers (adapter context glue) need marker comments so the semantic-boundary lint does not flag them as boundary violations.
Widget demo enforcement must read category files and check for the component name, not just verify file existence.
Widget smoke check visual assertions for unstyled plain examples should be skipped; color-delta checks only apply to CSS/Tailwind styled examples.

workflow: GitHub GraphQL API is required to resolve PR review threads; the REST API cannot resolve threads.
Sandbox environments may block outbound GitHub API calls; escalation outside the sandbox is needed for gh CLI operations.
When polling CI/review state, direct periodic `gh pr checks` + reaction + thread-count queries are more reliable than trusting notification delivery.
Commit message must include `Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>` trailer when addressing Codex review findings in this repo.
After resolving all Codex threads and pushing, a fresh `@codex review` comment must be posted to trigger the next review pass.
Coverage and Release Verification are the slowest CI lanes in this repo and should be the last expected to complete.

friction: Initial GitHub API calls failed silently from inside the sandbox, requiring escalation to run the same commands outside it.
Commit hook ran for over two minutes with no output, creating ambiguity about whether it was stuck or just slow.
Codex second-pass returned 6 new findings only after all CI lanes were nearly complete, requiring another full fix/commit/push/resolve/retrigger cycle.
The `prop:checked` Leptos binding and controlled reset callback fix required cross-referencing framework skill docs before editing, adding latency.
Patch context mismatches required re-locating insertion points in checkbox/mod.rs test section.
open_items: Coverage CI lane was still running when the session ended; final green status not confirmed.
Codex third-pass review outcome unknown — the second `@codex review` trigger was not yet complete at session end.
The widget category-file component-name check uses a simple string search; a more robust AST-based check could reduce false positives/negatives for aliased imports.

Framework-local callback wiring (Leptos/Dioxus callbacks not being Send+Sync) is a known limitation; a future refactor could explore a type-erased callback wrapper to unify adapter and core callback handling.
